articles.title: Viltrox AF 20mm F2.8 Lens Review: An Affordable Ultra-Wide Option
articles.content: Looking for an affordable full-frame lens? The Viltrox AF 20mm f/2.8 might be just what you need. Priced at $158, it offers great value while delivering ultra-wide-angle shots. In this review, we'll look at the lens's performance, build quality, image quality, and more. Whether you're a videographer or a photographer, this lens is worth considering. Let's see if it lives up to the hype! Performance and build quality The Viltrox AF 20mm f/2.8 may not be the most premium lens out there, but it holds up well in terms of construction. Despite its lightweight construction, the lens feels solid and durable. The plastic focus ring rotates smoothly, although the manual focus response is a bit slow. In terms of autofocus, the lens can be a bit sluggish in single-shot mode, but it picks up the pace in continuous autofocus mode. The lens also has a small 52mm filter thread, which requires thin filters to prevent physical vignetting. Overall, the build quality is commendable considering its affordability. Image quality and performance Sharpness Center sharpness Edge Sharpness Center sharpness is better at the full open aperture of F2.8, and edge sharpness is at its best when contracted to F5.6. Vignetting Vignetting is more noticeable without profiles, but video mode works with Sony and Nikon bodies to correct Vignetting , and photo mode can be easily corrected using profiles. Distortion Without using a profile, there is slight pincushion distortion in the distortion control, which can be corrected in post-processing by using a profile. Dispersion 20mm F2.8 dispersion in the case of shooting strong contrast objects without using profile is prone to dispersion, it is recommended to shrink the aperture in the outdoors Flare Proximity, dark environment will produce flare, long distance no obvious flare Coma Coma isn't great for professional stargazers, but for entry-level enthusiasts, the 20mm wide angle can be used as a backup lens Breathing Breathing effect is a detrimental factor in video shooting that affects picture stabilization, which is milder with the 20mm. The video will be shown at the end of the article Tips and considerations To get the best results with the Viltrox AF 20mm f/2.8 lens, it's recommended to stop down the aperture. This will ensure optimal sharpness and reduce potential distortion. It's also important to be aware of the lens' vignetting, especially when using filters. Choosing thin filters can help minimize this problem. In addition, the lens comes with a plastic hood that can help reduce purple flare and excessive white light. Keep these tips in mind to get the most out of this lens. Conclusion The Viltrox AF 20mm f/2.8 is undoubtedly an affordable option for those in need of an ultra-wide-angle lens. With its solid build quality and impressive image performance, it offers great value for money. While it does have some distortion and vignetting issues, these can be mitigated by stopping down the aperture and being mindful of filter usage. Whether you're into videography or photography, this lens is definitely worth considering. Step up your creative game with the Viltrox AF 20mm f/2.8 lens ! articles.title: Celebrating 90 Years of NIKKOR Excellence
articles.content: In the vast realm of photography, one name is synonymous with unparalleled optical quality and precision - NIKKOR. As we celebrate the 90th anniversary of this iconic brand, it's not just a reflection of decades past, but a testament to the relentless pursuit of excellence by Nikon, the visionary force behind NIKKOR. 1. The birth of NIKKOR: 1932 In 1932, the NIKKOR brand found its place in history when it was officially registered by Nikon, then known as Nippon Kogaku K.K. What began as a brand name would become a symbol of optical brilliance and innovation in the world of photography. 2. Taking to the skies: 1933 One year later, in 1933, the first shipment of Aero-Nikkor aerial photography lenses took flight, marking Nikon's pioneering step into the specialized field of aerial photography. This early foray demonstrated Nikon's commitment to pushing the boundaries of optical technology. 3. The lens that graced LIFE: Nikkor P-C 8.5cm F2 (1948) The year 1948 marked a significant milestone for NIKKOR with the release of the Nikkor P-C 8.5cm F2. Photographs taken with this lens found their way into the pages of the world-renowned LIFE magazine, elevating NIKKOR's reputation on the international stage. This was a turning point that solidified NIKKOR as a name to be reckoned with in the world of photography. 4. Pioneering Zoom Technology: Zoom-NIKKOR Auto 43-86mm f/3.5 (1963) In 1963, NIKKOR once again led the way with the introduction of the Zoom-NIKKOR Auto 43-86mm f/3.5. This standard zoom lens was a trailblazer, offering practical performance that paved the way for the evolution of zoom lenses in the years to come. 5. The speed of light: NIKKOR Z 58mm f/0.95 S Noct (2019) Fast forward to 2019, and NIKKOR reached new heights with the release of the NIKKOR Z 58mm f/0.95 S Noct. This flagship lens made history as the fastest lens in Nikon's arsenal, boasting an extraordinary aperture of f/0.95. This lens not only pushed the boundaries of what was optically possible, but also demonstrated Nikon's commitment to innovation and pushing the boundaries of technology. NIKKOR lenses have been the eyes through which countless stories have been told, and as we look to the future, we anticipate even more milestones in the ongoing saga of NIKKOR optical brilliance.

articles.title: Fujifilm's 2023 lineup review: The X-S20, GFX 100 II, and New Lenses
articles.content: In 2023, Fujifilm has once again wowed photography enthusiasts with its latest releases. In this comprehensive review, we delve into the key features and performance of two flagship cameras - the Fujifilm X-S20 and the GFX 100 II - and take a look at the exciting new lenses that have been added to Fujifilm's repertoire. Fujifilm X-S20: A mid-range wonder The Fujifilm X-S20 is a DSLR-style mirrorless camera designed to meet the needs of hybrid photography enthusiasts. Featuring a 26MP X-Trans BSI CMOS sensor, this camera takes a step beyond its predecessors with significant improvements in image stabilization, processing power, and autofocus capabilities. Key Features: 1. Sensor excellence: Equipped with a 26MP X-Trans BSI CMOS sensor, the X-S20 delivers exceptional image quality. 2. Design Evolution: Departing from Fujifilm's traditional X-series layout, the X-S20 features a more conventional control layout and larger grip for improved handling. 3. Extended battery life: With the ability to shoot approximately 800 frames per charge, the X-S20 ensures extended shooting sessions without worrying about running out of power. 4. Video Mastery: The X-S20 raises the bar for video recording with 6K/30p 4:2:2 10-bit internal recording, dedicated vlogging mode, auto scene detection and enhanced subject tracking. Market Feedback: 1. Tom's Guide: Describes the X-S20 as a phenomenal mid-range camera, praising its 6.2K/30p video, image quality, IBIS, build and design. However, they note the higher price and slightly awkward controls. 2. Digital photography review: Highlights the X-S20's flagship-level performance at a reasonable price, focusing on its versatile features for both photos and videos. 3. Camera Labs: Recognizes the X-S20 as a strong performer, improving stills capabilities and becoming one of the best in its price range for video. GFX 100 II: Speed, Autofocus and Film Simulation The GFX 100 II continues Fujifilm's legacy in medium-format photography with improved speed, autofocus capabilities, and notable additions on the video front. Key Features: 1. Speed and autofocus: The GFX 100 II boasts improved speed, autofocus capabilities and a new movie simulation mode: Reala ACE. 2. Handling improvements: Fujifilm addresses the handling shortcomings of the GFX100, creating a more rounded camera with a smaller, single-grip body reminiscent of the original GFX 50S. Market Feedback: 1. Digital Photography Review: Commends the improved speed, autofocus, and significant video improvements, including the new movie simulation mode. 2. Amateur Photographer: Praises the GFX 100 II for its updated autofocus and faster shooting, making it a versatile all-rounder. 3. PetaPixel: Commends the improved image quality and performance, but points out that speed comes at a price. New lenses from Fujifilm: In addition to the stellar cameras, Fujifilm introduces a trio of lenses, each designed to meet different needs and preferences. 1. XF 8mm f/3.5 R WR: A wide-angle lens designed for creative perspectives with a fisheye view suitable for various photographic styles. 2. GF 30mm f/5.6 T/S Lens: A tilt-shift lens that offers control over perspective and depth of field, ideal for architectural and landscape photography. 3. GF 55mm f/1.7 R WR Lens: A wide aperture prime lens that offers excellent low-light performance and beautiful bokeh for portrait enthusiasts. 4. GF 110mm f/5.6 T/S Macro Lens: Combining tilt-shift capabilities with macro photography, this lens offers versatility for capturing intricate details. Conclusion: With the 2023 lineup, Fujifilm reaffirms its commitment to innovation and excellence in the world of digital imaging. Whether you are an enthusiast looking for a mid-range wonder like the X-S20 or a professional entering the medium-format world with the GFX 100 II, Fujifilm continues to offer cutting-edge solutions. What's more, the new lenses add even more versatility, ensuring that there's something for every creative endeavor.

articles.title: Unveiling the Excellence of Viltrox UV Ultra-slim Filters
articles.content: In the realm of photography, the use of filters has become a nuanced art that enhances the quality and versatility of the images captured. Among the plethora of filters available, Viltrox UV filters stand out as indispensable tools for photographers seeking optimal lens protection and image clarity. In this blog post, we delve into the world of Viltrox UV filters, exploring their unique features and highlighting their importance in various shooting scenarios. Understanding filters: Filters come in a variety of shapes and serve a variety of functions, from square and circular filters to magnetic, rear-mount, built-in, mobile, aerial, and action camera filters. Of these, circular filters, especially front-mounted ones, are the most commonly used in everyday photography. Functionality of filters: Filters are categorized based on their functions, including UV protection filters, ND/GND/adjustable ND filters, CPL polarizing filters, black/white soft filters, focusing filters, anti-glare/narrow band filters, silk filters, infrared restoration filters, underwater filters, and more. What is a UV Filter? UV filters, originally designed for film and CCD cameras, were intended to block ultraviolet light below 390nm. With modern cameras incorporating anti-UV coatings, UV filters now primarily serve a protective role. Viltrox UV filters, equipped with advanced features, go beyond mere protection. Viltrox UV Filter Lens Features: 1. Double-sided Nano 18-layer high transmittance coating: With a remarkable 99.5% high light transmission and minimal 0.3% surface reflectance, Viltrox's nano coating ensures uniform light transmission, color balance and reduced surface reflections. The color of the coating, influenced by light of different wavelengths, minimizes reflection and presents a distinctive blue hue. 2. Hydrophobic and Oleophobic Coatings: The filter surface is treated with hydrophobic and oleophobic layers. The hydrophobic layer repels water, causing droplets to bead up and roll off without leaving a trace, while the oleophobic layer resists oil, maintaining a clean surface. 3. Lens matting process: Viltrox uses a professional matte black coating on the sides of the filter, coupled with high-precision spin coating. This minimizes stray light and significantly improves image clarity and quality. Viltrox UV Filter Frame Characteristics: 1. Aerospace grade precision aluminum alloy: Manufactured with precision CNC machining, the filter frame features light weight, high strength, corrosion resistance, and excellent heat and cold resistance. 2. 3.2mm Ultra-Thin Outer Ring Design: The 3.2mm ultra-thin outer ring is designed for use with wide-angle lenses, ensuring no vignetting. The filter's card slot structure ensures a secure fit, preventing lens misalignment and flaring. 3. Anti-slip grooves: The outer curvature of the filter frame features unique groove patterns that enhance grip during installation and removal, ensuring a comfortable user experience. Ideal shooting scenarios: Viltrox UV filters are essential in environments with snow, steam, seawater, fog, dust and sand. They protect against potential lens damage and allow for worry-free cleaning in challenging conditions. Non-ideal shooting scenarios: In situations with strong backlighting, especially when using telephoto lenses, UV filters can cause glare. For critical image quality requirements, it's advisable to remove the UV filter in such scenarios. Conclusion: Viltrox UV filters are proving to be essential companions for photographers, offering not only protection but also a range of advanced features to enhance image quality. Whether capturing the serenity of snowy landscapes or braving the harsh conditions of sandy deserts, Viltrox UV filters prove their worth in a variety of shooting scenarios. Enhance your photography with the precision and excellence of Viltrox UV filters.
articles.author: SUSIE SU

articles.title: FUJIFILM X100VI Launch: Classic Redefined, Features Upgraded
articles.content: In the realm of digital cameras, Fujifilm has always been a brand that commands attention. Its X-series cameras have garnered global acclaim for their exceptional image quality, user-friendly operation, and timeless design aesthetics. Now, Fujifilm has once again introduced a new member to its X-series lineup: the FUJIFILM X100VI, a compact digital camera that seamlessly integrates mature design principles with advanced technological features. Since the introduction of its first digital camera in 2011, Fujifilm's X100 series has enjoyed widespread popularity. And with the X100VI, the sixth-generation product in the series, Fujifilm continues to build upon its legacy of classic design and compact form factor while introducing a range of new functionalities and technologies to enhance the shooting experience for users. First and foremost, the X100VI boasts a brand-new 40.2-megapixel X-Trans™ CMOS 5 HR sensor paired with the X-Processor 5 high-speed image processing engine, delivering outstanding image quality and exceptional performance. When combined with the 23mm F2.0 lens, the camera fully leverages the high-resolution sensor to produce sharper and more vivid images. Additionally, the X100VI offers 20 film simulation modes, including the new "REALA ACE," providing users with a diverse range of shooting options to imbue each photo with personality and charm. Moreover, the X100VI introduces a new in-body image stabilization feature, offering up to 6.0 stops of stabilization compensation, significantly improving shooting stability in low-light conditions. The camera also supports various object detection autofocus functions, utilizing AI technology to accurately identify and track different subjects, thereby streamlining the shooting process for users. In terms of design, the X100VI retains the traditional styling of the X100 series, featuring an aluminum body that undergoes meticulous processing and anodization to achieve a premium look and feel. Button placements on the body have been optimized for enhanced usability, while the redesigned grip conforms better to ergonomic principles, ensuring a more comfortable handholding experience. In addition to the camera itself, Fujifilm offers a range of accessories such as adapters, protective filters, and conversion lenses, allowing users to expand their photographic horizons and meet the demands of diverse shooting scenarios.

Lens Communication: Some adapters maintain electronic communication between the lens and camera body. This allows for aperture control, autofocus, and metadata transmission. Viltrox PL-mount adapters often support electronic communication, enhancing convenience. Infinity Focus: Ensure the adapter allows for accurate infinity focus. Some adapters may require slight adjustments to achieve precise focus. Viltrox adapters are designed to maintain infinity focus. 4.
